input: A pricing page with three tiers: Hobby ($0/mo), Pro ($20/mo, marked as most popular), and Enterprise (custom pricing). Each tier should list 4-5 features with checkmark icons.

A dashboard sidebar with collapsible sections. Sections: Overview, Analytics (with sub-items: Traffic, Conversions, Revenue), Settings (with sub-items: Profile, Team, Billing), and a Sign Out button at the bottom.
A hero section for a B2B SaaS landing page. Bold headline, supporting paragraph, primary CTA button, secondary 'Watch demo' link with a play icon, and a small row of customer logos below.
output:
To create a hero section for a B2B SaaS landing page, I'll structure it with a bold headline, a supporting paragraph, a primary CTA button, a secondary "Watch demo" link with a play icon, and a row of customer logos. Here's how the code will look: ```tsx // components/HeroSection.tsx import React from 'react'; import { Button } from './ui/button'; import { Logo } from './ui/logo'; // Assuming you have a Logo component for customer logos const HeroSection: React.FC = () => { return (

Transform Your Business with Our SaaS Solution

Our platform streamlines your operations, enhances productivity, and drives growth. Join the future of business management today!

play_arrow Watch demo
); }; export default HeroSection; ``` ### Explanation: - **Headline**: A bold, attention-grabbing headline that communicates the value proposition. - **Supporting Paragraph**: A brief description that elaborates on the benefits of the SaaS solution. - **Primary CTA Button**: A button for users to get started, styled with a blue background and white text. - **Secondary Link**: A link to watch a demo, accompanied by a play icon for visual appeal. - **Customer Logos**: A row of logos representing satisfied customers, enhancing credibility. input: A data table for displaying users. Columns: avatar, name, email, role, status (active/inactive badge), last login, and an actions menu. Include a search bar above and pagination below.

A modal dialog for confirming a destructive action like deleting an account. Should have a warning icon, a clear title, descriptive body text, a Cancel button, and a red Delete button. Make the destructive nature visually obvious.
A settings page with a vertical tab navigation on the left (Profile, Account, Notifications, Security, Billing) and the corresponding content panel on the right. Show the Profile tab as active.

A file upload component with drag-and-drop. Should show a dashed border drop zone, an upload icon, instructional text, a 'browse files' link, and a list of currently uploaded files with progress bars and remove buttons.
